NMDCc

e Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the configured
scenarios in respect of new developments to be made.

e Preparation of the documents like As-is-study, Business Blue Print along with gap analysis and
get it approved through Core Team Members before starting to configuration of future business
processes in SAP.

e Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes in line with the
problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Support: ABAP

e Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

e Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields, selection
criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code change

e Maintenance of Customized Transactions

e Performance tuning

e SAP Note applications

e Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP

e Working closely with functional teams

e Support in analyzing & resolving the dump issues reported by the users

BASIS

e — Review and Analyse Daily, Weekly and Annual System Monitoring Checks.

e Assignment of Roles and Authorisations to end users as per approval regularly without delay
to ensure no business transactions was halted for want of Roles & Authorisations. (Like: CTS
move, User & Profile assignment and small correction in profile)

e Troubleshoot and rectify the problems on day-to-day basis

e Client copy / system refresh (whenever required at least once in a year)

e SAP note applications with reference to SAP Basis

e SNC configuration (whenever required at least once ina year).

e Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP Basis

e Performance Tuning on Periodic Basis

e Support will be extended for Data Migrations for the approved change requests. The upload
data formats will be provided by the Consultants. NMDC will provide the cleansed and
valjdated data to the Support team.

ANNEXURE - B
Scope of Work for the proposal of Selection of Service Provider for Annual
Maintenance Contract for SAP S/4 HANA at NMDC
The scope of work in this tender includes
> To provide all the services (onsite support) required for successful and uninterrupted
day to day operations/transactions, maintenance, update and upgrade of the SAP ERP
System at all locations for the following modules of SAP S/4 HANA and entire SAP
landscape implemented in NMDC Limited (NMDC) including developments to be made
as per the requirements of business notified from time to time.

a) FINANCE & CONTROLLING (FICO)

b) MATERIALS MANAGEMENT (MM) INCLUDING SUPPLIER RELATIONSHIP
MANAGEMENT (SRM)

c) PRODUCTION PLANNING (PP)

d) PLANT MAINTENACE (PM)

SCOPE OF WORK for SAP Support Service
The broad scope of the Functional and Technical consultant for SAP Support
Services including enhancement / customization of the SAP application in NMDC
mentioned hereunder. It may note that the scope mentioned hereunder is not
exhaustive:
SPECIFIC REQUIREMENT:
i. The successful bidder is required to provide technical & functional support
services which include the identification and rectification of all defects/bugs.

ii. The successful bidder is required to assist in providing solution for recovery
from transaction errors, system errors, hardware failures and/or other
application, modification, enhancement issues etc.

iii. The successful bidder is required to assist in performing period end closing
activities (Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually) including Tax reporting,
Regulatory Compliance etc.

iv. The support team should be based at NMDC Head Office, Hyderabad on full time
basis in respect of NMDC to facilitate timely resolution of issues.

v. Enhancements/developments, patches, version upgrades, service packs etc. (as
applicable) are to be installed time to time where ever required on release by
OEM in consultation with the company including third party solutions
implemented and integrated with SAP. Each upgrade should be accompanied by
a detailed document containing the step-by-step procedure for effecting the
changes to the system by ensuing an established change management process.

vi. The successful bidder shall review and adopt the Governance, Risk & Compliance
(GRC) in the system to streamline and have better control across the transactions
of the company.

vii. The Successful Bidder shall assign the Roles and Authorizations to end users as
and when required as per approval without delay to ensure no business
transactions was halted for want of Roles & Authorizations.

viii. The successful bidder shall provide contact numbers, email addresses, escalation
matrix of a dedicated support team. NMDC shall be kept informed well in advance
in case any changes are being made in the contact details.


---

Page 406 (pytesseract-ocr):
me 2354

ix. The successful bidder shall submit detailed documentation (e.g. Technical
documents, Configuration documents, Functional document if any) for each
resolved issue GM ERP, NMDC.

x. The Management Dashboards has been developed and configured through Qlik
Sense Software in respect of NMDC. The Scope of support comprises
maintenance of existing Dashboards including addition / deletion / modification
of specific views as per the business requirements.

xi. For changes pertaining to completely new processes, or any new developments
including Dashboards etc., the total efforts (man days) required for the change
requests will be mutually agreed by both the successful bidder and Owner. If the
total efforts required are up to 5 man days, then the successful bidder has to
implement the changes free of cost. In case of changes requiring efforts more
than 5 man days, the successful bidder has to obtain approval of Owner with
financial implications before carrying out the changes. The financial implication
will be calculated by reducing the agreed effort by 5 man days and then
multiplying the balance man days with the rate (per man days) quoted in the
commercial/price bid by the successful bidder. It also includes integration with
third party software planned in future, if any.

xii. The successful bidder has to develop a tracking system Module Wise to record
the issues from “raising of ticket” by the user / ERP Module Core Member till
providing resolution.

xiii. Computers/laptops for office work of staff of successful bidder shall be in the
scope of successful bidder. All other services required to administer, maintain,
update and upgrade the SAP ERP System as per standard of performance
described in the Tender are included in the scope of Services of successful bidder.

xiv. The successful bidder will maintain the SAP ERP System in such manner so as to
minimize operating cost and maximize the system availability.

xv. The successful bidder has to prepare various documents including
Administration Manual, User Manuals, Standard Operating Procedure, Standard
Maintenance Procedure, etc for SAP ERP System and SAP Software.

xvi. The successful bidder shall submit all the necessary documentation (i.e.,
Functional Specifications, Technical Specifications, List of affected codes, Test
Scripts, Unit Test Plans, System Integration Test, User Acceptance Test Plans etc.)
for any changes they perform in the system. All these documents should be
approved by NMDC.

xvii. The successful bidder also has to provide all temporary work, ancillary work,
enabling work etc. including provision of tools & software required for
performing Services.

xviii. The successful bidder shall provide adequate training as well as awareness

Role of SAP Functional Consultant

a. Assist business users for the problems/issues encountered during day-to-day
business transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System.

b. Study and evaluate the users’ requirements by interacting with the Core Team
Members & User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an
abstract and algorithmic business model or System Requirement Specifications.

c. Realize the solution through standard features of SAP, if standard feature or
alternative solution is not available, solution may be provided through
configuration and customizing the respective business area and ensure to map
the system to achieve the business requirements

d. Provide the Documentation for the settings and Standard Operating
Procedures (SOP) that allow other consultants to do further changes or
modifications if required.

e. Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever
required.

f. Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the
configured scenarios in respect of new developments to be made.

g. Preparation of the documents like As-is-study, Business Blue Print along with
gap analysis and get it approved through Core Team Members before starting
to configuration of future business processes in SAP.

h. Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes
in line with the problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Consultant: ABAP

a. Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

b. Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields,
selection criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code
change

c. Maintenance of Customized Transactions


---

oe 2349
Xe

d. Performance tuning

e. SAP Note applications

f. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP

g. Working closely with functional teams

h. Support in analyzing & resolving the dump issues reported by the users

Role of SAP Technical Consultant: BASIS

a. Review and Analyze Daily, Weekly, Monthly and Annual System Monitoring
Checks.

b. Assignment of Roles and Authorizations to end users as per approval regularly
without delay to ensure no business transactions was halted for want of Roles
& Authorizations. (Like: CTS move, User & Profile assignment and small
correction in profile)

d. Client copy / system refresh (whenever required at least once in a year)

e. SAP note applications with reference to SAP Basis

f. SNC configuration (whenever required at least once in a year).

g. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP Basis

h. Performance Tuning on Periodic Basis and ensure 99.9% uptime

i. Support will be extended for Data Migrations for the approved change requests.
The upload data formats will be provided by the Consultants. NMDC will provide
the cleansed and validated data to the Support team.

3.0 DETAILED SCOPE OF WORK:
It is proposed to implement integrated Software Solution through SAP S/4 HANA
ERP software for TANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO which will provide an
organization wide view of the operations and provide the management with
accurate, timely and reliable information for better decision making. The
implementation of ERP in the entire Distribution of TANGEDCO is covered under
the IPDS scheme. The implementation of ERP in the entire Generation except
new power projects and entire TANTRANSCO will be done under TNEB’s own
funds. This section outlines the Detailed Scope of Work for the Implementation
Partners, herein after referred to as the System Integrator for the above work.
The SI shall Understand TANGEDCO’s and TANTRANSCO’s business process,
design and development of implementation plan covering all functions, Sizing of
hardware to suit the requirement, Business Blue printing Configuration and
realization of the ERP solution, development of interfaces/ integration with the
legacy systems (as decided by TANGEDCO), testing, data migration/ porting,
technical support, Core team & Power User training (Train the trainer) for
functional and user training, final preparation for go-live , Switch-over to the ERP
solution, Post “Go-Live” support, documentation for Training ,Configuration and
Blueprint Document , Recommendation for technical infrastructure, Change
management , Project management and monitoring. The contractor will be
required to ensure designing, configuring, testing and implementation of ERP S/4
Hana software. The contractor will be required to ensure designing, configuring,
testing and implementing all the functionalities/processes/modules that are
required to be implemented. The scope of work also includes Project
management, Change management, Process improvement and other services as
detailed in this document and on the requirement at the sole discretion of
TANGEDCO. Contractor is required to critically review the purpose of
implementation and shall ensure inclusion of all other essential services (not
mentioned specifically) for achieving the objective within the time frame for
successful implementation.

The main objectives of the different process areas of SAP S/4 HANA ERP are
summarized in the following paragraphs.
Financial Accounting & Control Processes
Some of the broad requirements that the management of TANGEDCO and
TANTRANSCO would look forward to from a robust financial accounting package
are:
v Provide for passing of bills through the system through a robust accounts
payable process
v Provide for accounting for income through the system through an accounts
receivable process
v Provide for passing of journal entries and consolidation and finalization of
accounts through the system and generate financial statements
¥, Provide for generation of online financial statements for Management to take
appropriate decisions based on updated financial data
v The regulator, Tamil Nadu Electricity Regulatory Commission (TNERC) will decide
tariffs based on financial information submitted by TANGEDCO and
TANTRANSCO as a part of the usual Tariff / ARR / compliance reporting. A major
part of such reporting would need to be obtained from the Financial Accounting
System.
The existing accounting system cannot meet the above requirements and hence
a new accounting software solution is being envisaged. This process is broadly
defined to meet the key functionality in the following context:
a) Capability to generate various financial MIS reports aiding managerial
decision making as well as operations management
al b) Capability to comply various statutory requirements such as Company’s Act,
S\ags te uy —f TIncome Tax, GST etc.

“ine” @) Capability to generate Ind-As reports and other report preferred by
TANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO
; Procurement, Sales & Inventory Management Processes
TANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO are in the continuous process of procuring for
new, maintenance, replacement works etc. Significant time & amount is spent on
procuring the materials (through vendor selection, evaluation, bidding processes,
Lite gine. etc.), operation & maintenance expenditure of the projects, Inventory and scrap

Interfacing with the legacy systems of TANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO.
The non-SAP external systems will need to be interfaced with the relevant
modules of SAP. The Si is expected to make a detailed study of the existing IT
systems (island software) available in TANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO and set up
and integration plan to integrate the various areas with the ERP system,
primarily for the purpose of Financial Accounting.
Other requirements — Document / file management System
TANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO intends to implement a Document / file system
which shall store all internal and external documents, files etc. along with the
respective SAP Transaction. The objective of this implementation is to reduce
paper work and also ensure that all relevant documents are stored electronically
along with the respective SAP transaction. Such documents could also include
technical literature, plan documents, diagrams and software files.
The System Integrator will need to carry out the following activities:
1) Install and implement the above modules of SAP ERP software and anything
incidental there upon.
2) Design & prepare test data, trial run and arrange user acceptance testing
(UAT) of all modules
3) Integration with other legacy modules of the systems at TANGEDCO and
TANTRANSCO
4) Data migration from the legacy systems
5) Core Team Training
6) Change Management Implementation

The SI shall provide the sizing for the servers, storage and networking
equipments, architecture, landscape and generic technical specification for the
servers and allied hardware proposed to be procured by TANGEDCO and
commissioned in the data centre and the disaster recovery centre of TANGEDCO
for implementation of SAP S/4 HANA ERP software. The SI should specify the
hardware & networking configuration and the specifications most suitable for
running the SAP ERP software. The service provider will be responsible for
ensuring that all hardware and networking specifications are properly drawn up
to ensure that the software installation and its deployment are smooth across all
locations. Specifications shall be independent of any proprietary technology of
any hardware / networking vendor. Specific activities that need to be done in this
task are:

1) Provide sizing for servers, storage and networking equipment, architecture,
landscape and generic technical specification.

2) Provide TANGEDCO with the generic tender specifications for procuring the
hardware;

3) Assist TANGEDCO in carrying out a Quality check of the procured hardware,
storage and networking equipment;

4) Be a part of the team to achieve an effective installation of the hardware and
networking equipment;

5) Fine tune and optimize the hardware and networking facility during
implementation period.

6) While proposing new hardware from implementation of SAP ERP, existing
hardware to the extent possible should be utilized.

7) Draw hardware and software requirements for disaster recovery and business
continuity planning including system protection software like Anti-Virus, backup,
etc.

Deliverables for Task A shall include:-

1) Preparation, submission & sign off on hardware & network requirement

specifications for the data centre and the disaster recovery centre.

|" ee" Before carrying out the work of Configuration and customization, the SI shall prepare a
System Design and Project Plan. The detailed project plan of the implementation is to
be prepared at the commencement of the project. The implementation of ERP solution
will require a strong program management activity integrating the core product
implementation with software application. This ensures complete acceptability of the
solution within the organization. Program management services shall aim at improving
the business processes, leveraging technology solutions, incorporating best-of-breed
industry practices to ee a for TANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO's

business operations and at the same time addressing growth and development of the
employees.
b) Detailed Design
The SI shall thoroughly study the ‘AS-IS’ process mapping to activity/ task level and
current state analysis to identify areas of improvement and opportunities for
standardization. The SI shall review the ‘AS-IS’ processes and define the TO-BE
processes that are based on business requirements, GAP Analysis and ERP enabled
“best practices” and processes. The SI shall Design the specific screens and data
formats along with the process and method for entering the details as required. Based
on the functionalities designed, SI shall be responsible for:
> Configuring the ERP system according to the processes.
> Integration of the detailed design across modules.
> Testing of configuration of Modules.
> Identification of the fields that need to be captured for the Masters and mapping
of the information with the legacy systems.
The design proposed by SI, shall be realistic, simple, flexible enough to take care of
future changes in the organizational structure and easy maintainability.
c) Develop detailed scope for the functionalities to be implemented
The SI shall finalize the detailed scope of the business processes to be implemented in
association with the project team members from TANGEDCO during Blue Printing.
d) Deployment of Manpower
The entire development activities should be carried out in TANGEDCO HQ premises.
Required space will be allotted for the development team. The SI shall ensure
deployment of sufficient specialized, competent and experienced and dedicated man
power throughout the project to complete the implementation & stabilization of the
System in time successfully. The resources that have been proposed for this project
shall be fully deployed at TANGEDCO. SI should ensure the availability of the ERP
solution/Domain expert availability at TANGEDCO office whenever required.

outlined. The SI must ensure deployment of necessary resources and tools during the
testing phases. The SI shall take remedial action based on outcome of the tests. The SI
is expected to prepare the detailed test specification for each functional module which
shall cover the complete features, reports, master data, configuration and validation

TASK D — Implementation including Installation and roll out
Installation and roll out would be the actual Go Live phase for the package
implementation. This will cover the following activities:

1) Carrying out the final User Acceptance testing of the configured software and
obtaining the user and IT level sign-offs. This will include documentation of the
testing procedures and results obtained.

2) Transferring the duly tested configuration to the production environment.

3) Organizing Data Migration — the contractor must depute one person per location
to facilitate smooth data collection

4) Installation of the software in the production environment.

5) Planning for cut-over and assisting the client in carrying out cut-over procedures.

6) Data upload for opening balances and open master data with due validation and
checklists. Documentation of the data uploads to be maintained.

7) Assist in clearing the initial transactions.

8) Carry out the roll out of the software across all the locations in a time bound
\ manner.
NF 9) Hands on full support at the HQ and the field locations for a period of one
ad quarter, after the complete roll out, at all locations. The outputs shall be the
finalized MIS statements for the quarter duly approved by the client. This will
require service provider personnel to be deployed at the HQ / field offices of
FANGEDCO and TANTRANSCO;

10)Development of the necessary MIS Reports — generation of these reports for the
quarter which will be duly approved by the client;

11)Implement all designed access security and data validation controls;

1) A total support period of 3 years (36 months) after the stabilization period. This

of one expert for each module (MM, HR, Finance, ABAB and BASIS) on a full time

full first level implementation support, the contractor shall provide 2 years (24
months) of offsite implementation support which will include online 24 hour
access to software support services to resolve software related issues, bugs and
version management issues. In case of critical issues one engineer should be
available onsite to resolve the issues at the earliest. In the absence of the onsite
engineer necessary alternate arrangements should be made. Otherwise a penalty
of Rs.1000 per day per personnel will be deducted in the quarterly payment.

e Critical: Show stopper, Application breakdown/ crash, has serious implications on
running the production server and has affected or may affect greater than 50% of
users.

e High: Serious degradation of application performance, has impacted majority of
business processes, risk of significant data loss, affected or may affect around 10-
50% of users.

e Medium: Moderate degradation in application performance, no implications on data
integrity, had minimal or no impact on day-to-day operations and has affected or
may affect less than 10% of users.

e Low: Applications are stable and has no impact on day to day workings and has
affected or might affect a single user

e If the Uptime of ERP system is less than 65% for 2 consecutive quarters,
TANGEDCO shall have the right to terminate the Agreement. TANGEDCO shall have
the right to forfeit the Bank Guarantee and blacklist the contractor for any further
assignments in the state of TAMILNADU and simultaneously inform Central and
Other State Governments.

e The performance will be measured for each of the defined service level against the
target service level requirements and the violations will be calculated accordingly.

e The number of violations in the reporting period for each level of severity will be
totalled and used for the calculation of Penalties.

e If Selected Contractor feels that penalty imposed during a particular period is
unjustified/ attributed to uncontrollable reasons, then the Selected Contractor will
have the liberty to approach TANGEDCO for sorting out the issues. Till the time, the

. decision is taken, it will be considered as a penalty only. In the process if any
Wie, UF \\)ke amount is found deducted in excess, the same would be adjusted during subsequent
Rica Cerra

e Decision of TANGEDCO will be deemed final in any such, above mentioned concerns.

e It is to be noted that if the overall penalty applicable for any of the review period
during the period of the contract exceeds 25% or if the overall penalty applicable for
any of the successive quarter during the contract is above 25%; then TANGEDCO
shall have the right to terminate the contract.

4.0. PRICE :
The price accepted shall be kept firm during the entire period of this contract.
5.0 GST:
5.1. TANGEDCO has been registered as a dealer under GST Act 2017
(Registration No. 33AADCT4784E1ZC.)
5.2 GST is applicable @ 18% as per clause 2.0 (Schedule of materials and
rate). The GST will be paid as applicable subject to statutory variation if any
during the scheduled delivery period.
5.3 In case of delayed delivery, the GST prevailing on the actual date of
delivery or the GST on the last day of the contractual delivery period whichever is
LESS will be admitted. For both the cases the supplier shall furnish documentary
evidence while submitting the bills for payment.
5.4. Provided always, it is hereby agreed and declared that in case, where the
TANGEDCO has doubt whether GST is not at all payable for the transaction in
question, the TANGEDCO reserves the right to with-hold the payment of tax
until the party produces an order of Court of competent jurisdiction, declaring the
liability of the transaction to GST.
5.5. The GST Registration Certificate shall be submitted for approval in order to
admit GST.
5.6. TDS under GST is applicable for this purchase order and will be deducted @
City, ; 2% on each and every invoice of the contract as per Section 51 of CGST Act 2017
Teag sa 6 4° notified by the Central Government notification No.50/2018 dt. 13.9.18.

prescribed standards. These powers to withhold payments shall be without
prejudice to any other power/right of the purchaser under this contract.

amount that is appropriated will not be refunded to the contractor.

In case of failure to submit such extended Bank guarantee within the due date
(expiry date), TANGEDCO shall invoke the bank guarantee by addressing the bank
directly. Further, in the case of bank guarantee being extended without any break
in period and there is delay in submission of such extended bank guarantee, for
any reasons, TANGEDCO shall reserve the right to accept the belated submission of
the extended bank guarantee by levying penal interest at the rate of 22% per
annum for the delayed period of submission. Such penal interest shall be recovered
from the SI’s subsequent bill.

completion of support period and closure of P.O.

16.0. LIQUIDATED DAMAGES:
16.1 The execution of the contract as specified should be guaranteed by the contractor
under the Liquidated Damages Clauses given below:
16.2 If the contractor fails to complete the work within each milestone specified in the
completion period of the contract or any extension thereof, TANGEDCO shall recover
from the contractor as LD a sum of ¥ percent (0.5%) of the amount in each bill for the
delayed period if any, for completion of each milestone, for every completed week of
delay. The total liquidated damages shall not exceed ten percent (10%) of the contract
price of the items so delayed. Liquidated damages will be recovered from each bill with
delayed portion of work. The above LD is payable by the contractor only when the
delay is attributable on the part of contractor and not that of TANGEDCO.
16.3 The defaulting contractors will be made liable to pay to TANGEDCO in addition to
liquidated damages for delay, the actual difference in price, wherever TANGEDCO
orders the delayed quantity to be executed by other agencies at a higher rate. This is
without prejudice to other rights under the terms of contract.
16.4 If activities to be rendered against this Contract Order are completed by the
contractor beyond the Completion Period /delivery period stipulated in the order and if
they are accepted by TANGEDCO, such acceptance is without prejudice to TANGEDO's
rights to levy liquidated damages for the delay in completion.
16.5 TANGEDCO will also be at liberty to cancel the order if the execution is not made
as per the completion /delivery period specified in the order, notwithstanding its rights
to claim liquidated damages for the belated completion and incompletion of the
activities as on the date of cancellation.
16.6 The contractors are liable to pay the amount of loss sustained by TANGEDCO in
the event of non-execution of orders, if any placed on them either in full or part to the
satisfaction of TANGEDCO under the terms and conditions of contract and in the event
of placing orders for such quantities on some others at a higher price.

17.0 WARANTY:
17.1 Subject to Commercial terms & Conditions Clause 27, the Supplier further
warrants that the installation & implementation of SAP ERP Software shall be free from
defects arising from any act or omission of the Supplier or arising from design,
materials, and workmanship, under normal use in the conditions prevailing in the
country of final destination.
17.2 The warranty shall remain valid for twelve (12) months after the issue of
completion certificate by the competent authority for the contract.
17.3 TANGEDCO shall give Notice to the Supplier stating the nature of any such defects
together with all available evidence thereof, promptly following the discovery thereof.
TANGEDCO shall afford all reasonable opportunity for the Supplier to inspect such
defects.
17.4Upon receipt of such Notice, the Supplier shall, within One Month,
expeditiously repair or replace the defective installation & implementation of SAP
ERP Software or parts thereof, at no cost to TANGEDCO. If the defects are not
rectified or replaced within this period, penalty will be levied at a cost of Rs
5000/- per week of delay and the amount would be deducted from the amount
pending with TANGEDCO.
17.5 If having been notified, the Supplier fails to remedy the defect within the
period specified, TANGEDCO may proceed to take within a reasonable period
such remedial action as may be necessary, at the Supplier’s risk and expense and
without prejudice to any other rights which TANGEDCO may have against the
Supplier under the Contract.

21 .0. FORCE MAJEURE:

21.1 The contractor shall not be liable for delay in performing his obligations resulting

directly or from any force majeure conditions herein defined as:

(a) Any cause which is beyond the reasonable control of the contractor or TANGEDCO

as the case may be

(b) Natural phenomena, such as floods, drought, earthquakes and epidemics.

© Act of any Govt. Authority, domestic or foreign, such as wars declared or undeclared

quarantines, embargoes licensing control on production or distribution restrictions.

(d) Accident and disruptions such as fire, explosion, break down of essential machinery

or equipment etc.

(e) Strikes, slow down, and lockouts.

21,2 Failure or delay in the contractor's source of supply due to force majeure Causes

enumerated at 21.1. (a) to 21.1.(e) above shall be considered, provided the contractor

produces documentary evidence to show that there were no other alternative source of
supply available to him or if available the lead time required was likely to be longer than
the duration of the force majeure at the normal source of supply.

All the provisions of this clause shall apply whether the disruption cause is total or

partial in its effect upon the ability of the contractor to perform.

NOTE: The cause of force majeure condition will be taken into consideration only if the
contractor within 15 days from the occurrence of such delay notifies. TANGEDCO
shall verify the facts and grant such extension as the facts justify. For extension
due to force majeure conditions, the contractor shall submit his representation with
documentary evidence for scrutiny by TANGEDCO and decision of TANGEDCO shall
be binding on the firm.

a period exceeding 60 days, TANGEDCO may at its option terminate the contract by a

22.0 CHANGE ORDERS AND CONTRACT AMENDMENTS:
22,1 TANGEDCO may at any time order the Supplier through Notice in writing ,
to make changes within the general scope of the Contract in any one or more of
the following:
a. drawings, designs, or specifications, where installation & implementation of
SAP ERP Software to be furnished under the Contract are to be specifically
manufactured for TANGEDCO;
b. the method of shipment or packing if applicable;
c. the place of delivery; and
d. the Services to be provided by the Supplier.
22.2 If any such change causes an increase or decrease in the cost of, or the
time required for, the Supplier’s performance of any provisions under the
Contract, an equitable adjustment shall be made in the Contract Price or in the
Delivery and Completion Schedule, or both, and the Contract shall accordingly be
amended. Any claims by the Supplier for adjustment under this Clause must be
asserted within twenty-eight (28) days from the date of the Supplier's receipt of
TANGEDCO’'s change order.
22.3 Prices to be charged by the Supplier for any Related Services that might be
needed but which were not included in the Contract shall be agreed upon in
advance by the parties and shall not exceed the prevailing rates charged to other
parties by the Supplier for similar services.
22.4 The service provider will have to serve a notice of 8 weeks for any addition
or depletion of the resources.
a. During depletion the service provider should replenish this resource and have
an overlap of at least 4 weeks with the resources exiting the project.
b. The resource replacing the existing resource will be free of cost for during
aforesaid overlapping 4 weeks.
23.0 EXTENSIONS OF TIME:
23.1 If at any time during performance of the Contract, the Supplier should
encounter conditions impeding timely delivery of the _ installation &
implementation of SAP ERP Software pursuant to Commercial terms & Conditions
Clause 8, the Supplier shall promptly notify TANGEDCO in writing of the delay, its
likely duration, and its cause. As soon as practicable after receipt of the
s ~“<Supplier’s notice, TANGEDCO shall evaluate the situation and may at its discretion
\ 4 extend the Supplier's time for performance, in which case the extension shall be
IN Vv} ratified’ by the parties by amendment of the Contract.
iti 23.2 Except in case of Force Majeure, as provided under Commercial terms &
Conditions Clause 21, a delay by the Supplier in the performance of its Delivery
and Completion obligations shall render the Supplier liable to the = ge of

an extension of time is agreed upon, pursuant to Commercial terms & Conditions
Clause 23.
23.3 Failure on the part of supplier to deliver the installation & implementation of
SAP ERP Software within the stipulated period set out in the contract will attract
Liquidated Damage @ 0.5% per week of delay or part thereof on the value of the
undelivered portion, subject to a maximum of 10% of the contract value. If the
period of delay exceeds the specified maximum, the purchaser may consider
termination of the contract.
23.4 For the purpose of Penalty, the date quoted for completion of the task
supported by a Certificate issued by Competent Authority will be reckoned as the
date of delivery. The number of days of delay should be rounded to the nearest
week.
23.5 Penalty may not be levied in case of delay due to Force Majeure events or
other reasons agreed by the competent authority.
24.0 FAILURE TO EXECUTE THE CONTRACT:
24.1 If the successful tenderer herein after called the “Contractor” neglects to execute
the works with due diligence and expedition or refuses or neglects to comply with any
reasonable orders given to him, in writing by the Owner (TANGEDCO) in connection
with the works or contravene the provision of the contract, the owner (TANGEDCO)
may give notice in writing to the Contractor to make good the failure, neglect or
contravention complained of. Should the Contractor fail to comply with the notice within
thirty(30) days from the date of service thereof or the date stipulated for compliance,
then and in such case the Owner(TANGEDCO) shall be at a liberty to employ other
workmen and forthwith execute such part of the works as the Contractor may have
neglected to do or if the Owner (TANGEDCO) shall think fit, it shall be lawful for him,
without prejudice to any other right they may have under the contract, to take the
works wholly or in the part out of the Contractor hands and re-contract with any other
person or persons to complete the works or any part thereof, and in that event ,the
owner (TANGEDCO) shall have free use of the Contractor's facilities that may have been
at the time on the site in connection with the works without being responsible to the
Contractor for wear and tear thereof ,and to the exclusion of the right of the Contractor
over the same ,and the Owner (TANGEDCO) shall be entitled to retain and apply any
balance which may otherwise be due on the Contractor or such part thereof ,as may be
necessary, to the payment of the cost of executing the said part of the works or of
n completing the works as the case may be .If the cost of completing the works are
* *.°  @xecuting’ a part thereof ,as aforesaid shall exceed the balance due to the Contractor,

24.3 The support period shall be strictly in accordance with the details described in the
scope of work (Task E) The contractor shall depute personnel with adequate knowledge of
the modules and attend to the defects then and there. The contractor shall set up a full-
fledged helpdesk facility. The contractor shall work out the helpdesk operating procedures
and implement them jointly with client staff. If the support of the contractor during the
support period is not found satisfactory to TANGEDCO or if the contractor fails to extend
support when required, TANGEDCO shall deduct a portion of payment for non-
performance / non-satisfactory performance.
25.0. TERMINATION:
25.1 Termination for Default
(a) TANGEDCO, without prejudice to any other remedy for breach of
Contract, by Notice of default sent to the Supplier, may terminate the
Contract in whole or in part:
(i) if the Supplier fails to deliver any or all of the installation &
implementation of SAP ERP Software within the period specified in the
Contract, or within any extension thereof granted by TANGEDCO pursuant
to Commercial terms & Conditions Clause 23; or
(ii) if the Supplier fails to perform any other obligation under the Contract.
(b) In the event TANGEDCO terminates the Contract in whole or in part,
pursuant to Commercial terms & Conditions Clause 25, TANGEDCO
may procure, upon such terms and in such manner as it deems
appropriate, installation & implementation of SAP ERP Software similar
to those undelivered or not performed, and the Supplier shall be liable
to TANGEDCO for any additional costs for such similar installation &
implementation of SAP ERP Software. However, the Supplier shall
continue performance of the Contract to the extent not terminated.
25.2 Termination for Insolvency
TANGEDCO may at any time terminate the Contract by giving Notice to the
Supplier if the Supplier becomes bankrupt or otherwise insolvent. In such
event, termination will be without compensation to the Supplier, provided
that such termination will not prejudice or affect any right of action or
remedy that has accrued or will accrue thereafter to TANGEDCO.
25.3 Termination for Convenience
AN Ch, a. TANGEDCO, by Notice sent to the Supplier, may terminate the Contract,
“yy Ori in whole or in part, at any time for its convenience. The Notice of
= ‘_~-termination shall specify that termination is for TANGEDCO’s convenience,

terminated, and the date upon which such termination becomes eu

28.0 SETTLEMENT OF DISPUTES:
28.1 TANGEDCO and the Supplier shall make every effort to resolve amicably by
direct informal negotiation any disagreement or dispute arising between them
under or in connection with the Contract.
28.2. TANGEDCO will not accept any arbitration in case of disputes arising in any
respect under this contract, Any dispute arising out of this contract shall not be
subject to arbitration under the provisions of Arbitration and Conciliation Act
1996 in the event of any dispute between the Parties.

29.0 ASSIGNMENT:

Neither TANGEDCO nor the Supplier shall assign, in whole or in part, their
obligations under this Contract, except with prior written consent of the other
party. Dp

30.0 JURISDICTION FOR LEGAL PROCEEDINGS:
No suit or any proceedings in regard to any matter arising in respect of this
contract shall be instituted in any court, save in the High Court, Madras, City Civil
Court at Chennai or at the Court of small causes at Chennai. It is agreed that no
other court shall have jurisdiction to entertain any suit or proceedings even
though, part of the cause of action might arise within their jurisdiction. In case
any part of the cause of action might arise within the jurisdiction of any other
Courts in Tamil Nadu and rest within the jurisdiction of courts outside the Tamil
Nadu, then it is agreed to between the parties that such suits or proceedings
shall be instituted in a Court within the State of Tamil Nadu and no other court
outside the State of Tamil Nadu shall have jurisdiction even though any part of
the cause of action might arise within the jurisdiction of such courts. The
successful Tenderer shall furnish an undertaking as per Section XIII Contract
Forms in a non-judicial stamp paper of Rs.80/- agreeing to the above condition.
31.0 GENERAL:
This contract order binds all Other clauses stipulated in the tender
specification no. CC-25 of 18-19.
32.0. CORRESPONDENCE :
Your acknowledgement of receipt of this order may be sent within a week from
the date of receipt of Contract order and contract and correspondence on all
technical as well as other matters may be addressed to the Chief Engineer/IT
144, Anna Salai, Chennai-2.

A Provisional tax credit is effected only for TDS / TCS Statements filed by Government deductors,“P” status will be changed to Final (F) on
verification of payment details submitted by Pay and Accounts Officer (PAO)
In case of non-government deductors, payment details of TDS / TCS deposited in bank by deductors have matched with the payment
F Final details tientioned in the TDS / TCS statement filed by the deductors. In case of government deductors, details of TDS / TCS booked in
JGovernment account have been verified with payment details submitted by Pay & Accounts Officer (PAO)
Payment details of TDS / TCS deposited in bank by deductor have matched with details mentioned in the TDS / TCS statement but the
) lOverbooked amount is over claimed in the statement. Final (F) credit will be reflected only when deductor reduces claimed amount in the statement or
makes additional payment for excess amount claimed in the statement

2.0 PRICES:
The Unit FORD price mentioned at clause No.l) of “Schedule of
Material & Prices” are F.O.R. destination prices and are FIXED &
FIRM during the contract period. All supplies and services required for
the Contract are priced inclusive of all incidental costs {such as out of
pocket expenses, office suppcrt, communications, printing of
materials, etc.) and inchisive of supply, packaging, forwarding, freight,
transit, storage, insurance charges, all taxes, duties & ather charges.
3.0 TAXES, DUTIES & LEVIES APPLICABLE
(i) SALES TAX
The Unit FORD price mentioned at Sr. No, (1) in table under clause
No.2(A) of “Schedule of Material & Prices” above ure inclusive of all
taxes. Current applicable rate of CST is 5%. Any statutory veriation in
the sales tax will be to the supplier's account.
ii) SERVICE TAX:
The Service tax us mentioned at clause No.1{A) of “Schedule of
Materia! & Prices" above, shall be borne by the purchaser. Current
applicable rate of service tax inclusive of all CESS is 12.36%, Any
Statutory variation in the service tax will be to the supplier's account.
The Supplier shall give Service Tax registration number, The Service
Tax will be deducted at source on reverse charge basis as per the
applicable laws. Depositing the Service Tax with appropriate authority
within stipulated penod shall be the supplier's liability,
(i) ANNUAL TECHNICAL SUPPORT (ATS) CHARGES
The Annual Technical Suppurt Charges at Sr. No. [2) in table under
clause No.1 (Aj of "Schedule of Material & Prices" above, shall be borne
by the purchaser. Current applicable rate of Annual Technical
= oe? Support (ATS) 1s 22% on unit price of SAP WPB. Any statutory
ate pia xeiiation in this will be to the supplier's account.

14.0 DATA CONVERSION AND MIGRATION
The data shall be migrated in the prapased SAP Solution from the
legacy system Le. IMIS. project and/or provided in saft copy. The
supplier shall undertake the testing of the data conversion and
migration software as part of system integration testing. The supplier
shall develop all the requisite data conversion programs to convert
data from legacy system/exeel sheet format to SAP BRP upload
format. RVPN personne! shall prepare the data in the required upload
format. The Supplier should also develop data transfer routines for
data upload to SAP ERP. The exact format, frequency and granularity
af data should Se prepared by Supplier and agreed with RVPN core
team curing the implementation,
Supplier's responsibility for Data conversion &Migration:
This shall include the following tasks:
* Assistance in Checking data quality, cleansing and integrity,
* Creation of new/additional data mandatory in the new system,
* Preparing and validating data mapping structure,
* Identification & development of programs for data upload;
download,
* Providing data migration tools and templates to RVPN,
* Populate and migrate all the legacy) raw/new datw element to the
SAP ERP system.
* Import data from various formats (text, excel, CSV, XML ete,},
* Integration testing of the configured system using the populated
master and transaction data,
* Training and facilitating RVPN Core team & IT team for this
purpose,
* Assist RVPN team in Master Data management.
The data to be migrated shall be decided on mutually agreed and need
Renee basis such as;

25.0 LIQUIDATED DAMAGES
The implementation of SAP ERP Solution shall be considered as
successfully completed when the Project Manager has issued
operational acceptance certificate. If the Supplier fails to attain
campletion of praject within the time frame specified in the contract or
uny extension in the contract, due to reasons not attributed te the
purchaser, the purchaser shall recover the amount of LD, in lieu of
penalty, @ 0.5% of the total contract price, paid or payable to the
Supplier per week of delay or part thereof up to the maximum of 10%
of the contract price paid or payable to the Supplicr,
Purther, these shall be governed by the provisions of clause No. 18 of
thé Genera! Condition of Contract.

2.0 PRICES
The base price mentioned at clause No.1 of “Schedule of Prices” is FIXED
& FIRM during the contract period. Total price (in Column “F” in clause
(1) above) is inclusive of all taxes, duties & other charges.
3.0 TAXES & DUTIES
Current applicable rate of GST is 18%. Any statutory variation in the
GST will be to the firm's account. The Firm shall provide GST
registration number to RVPN. Depositing the GST with appropriate
authority within stipulated period shall be the firm's liability.
4.0 INSURANCE
The Firm shall take and maintain in effect adequate insurance policies
for their personnel for accident, injuries and loss of life, if any. Firm shall
observe all applicable regulations regarding safely on site. The
purchaser shall have the full powers to stop the work if any activity is
found unsafe.

8.0 SCOPE OF WORK
The broader scope of implementation of GST in RVPN shall be as under -
A. Master Data (Create/Change/Addition) & configuration
i. Chart of Accounts
ii. Material Master
iii. Service Master
iv. Customer Master
v. Vendor Master
vi. Condition Records etc.
vii. Modifications in TAXINN tax procedure
viii. Creation of Condition Types
ix. Configuration of new pricing schemas for procurement and sales
x. Creation of Business Places
xi. Mapping of Business Places to Plants
xii. Configuration of account determination for GST postings
xiii. |New Tax Codes and alteration in old Tax Code (if any)
xiv. Any other Master Data (if required)
All master data shall be reviewed and appropriate changes will be
suggested by M/s. YASH team. It includes preparation of Master Data
templates for changes in various master data according to GST.
B. Materials Management
a) Procurement of Materials — Domestic (Interstate/ Intrastate)
b) Procurement of Services — Domestic (Interstate/ Intrastate)
c) Procurement of Materials — Import
.d) Procurement of Services - Import

E. Basis

F. Others

Testing, Integration Testing for GST related processes.
b) Analyzing of open transactions (PO & SO) migration and impact (if
any) to be provided to RVPN.
c) Migration templates for open transactions (Contract, PO & SO)
according to GST.
d) Cut over preparation and Go-Live
e) Post Go-live Support

GST implementation on TRM, PS, PM and HCM (If any or required)
as applicable to ensure accurate accounting and reporting in all
respects.
g) Configuration of unique sequential numbering for outgoing GST
invoices, Bill of Supply, Debit Note, Credit Note, Supplementary
Invoice, Advance Voucher, Receipt Voucher or any further document
as per the directives of tax authority.
Any other requisite to fulfill the implementation of GST for SAP ERP in
RVPN shall be firm’s responsibility and cost if any occurs, the same shall
only be borne by the firm.

10.0 TRAINING
RVPN requires end user training for technical (E&M, Civil, MPT&S,
Comm., Legal etc.) and accounts wing for around 80-100 people. The
training shall be carried out at Jaipur Location. The appropriate
duration may be mutually decided by RVPN & firm. Training facilities
such as hardware, software, furniture, refreshment etc. shall be provided
by RVPN.
11.0 TESTING
The firm should define the Integration test scripts. These test scripts will
be used for testing, based on review and approval of RVPN. The firm
should populate sample master data in the system and configure the
data integration. The firm shall undertake the testing of the data
conversion and migration to SAP system. The firm shall undertake the
complete testing of the entire new system. The Integration testing will be
done jointly by the firm’s implementation team and the RVPN core team.
12.0 USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING
The firm shall define the User Acceptance Test (UAT) scripts. The
Purchaser will approve the UAT test scripts. User Acceptance Testing
(UAT) will be performed by the core team user at RVPN site, in
accordance with approved UAT plan.
13.0 OPERATIONAL ACCEPTANCE
As soon as all the prerequisites have been completed by the firm for sign
off in accordance with the provisions of the Specifications been
completed and is ready for sign off for Operational Acceptance, the firm
B ih Fi shall notify the purchaser in writing. If, the purchaser is satisfied with
Phe “© complete work, operational acceptance certificate will be issued. With the
ee issue of Operational Acceptance Certificate, the facilities shall be deemed
; to be Taken Over by the RVPN.
Further, these shall be governed by the provisions of clause No. 31 of the
= General Condition of Contract.

16.0 SIGN-OFF AND CLOSURE
RVPN Team will coordinate in expediting the process of sign-off and
closure of agreed deliverables/milestones. However, the firm shall
facilitate such acceptance/sign-off from the Purchaser for all the
deliverables by way of preparing / producing required documentation /
review reports / test results etc. as may be necessary for RVPN to
ascertain that the prerequisites to subject sign-off and closure have been
met completely in accordance of the scope of work.
17.0 TERMS OF PAYMENTS
The firm’s request for payment shall be made to the Purchaser in
writing, accompanied by an invoice (in triplicate) describing, as
appropriate, the milestone completed along with relevant supporting
documents.
The payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of submission of an
invoice/claim complete in all respects & supported by the requisite
documents and fulfilment of stipulated conditions & contractual
formalities by the firm after due verification by the Purchaser, who shall
forward the same to the Sr. AO (CPC).
All payments shall be made to the Firm by the Sr. AO (CPC), RVPN,
Jaipur through electronic payment mechanism for which necessary
details shall be furnished by the Firm during execution of the Contract.
No interest shall be paid on delayed payments.

18.0 LIQUIDATED DAMAGES
The implementation of GST in SAP ERP Solution shall be considered as
successfully completed when the Purchaser has issued operational
acceptance certificate. If the Firm fails to attain completion of project
within the time frame specified in the contract or any extension in the
contract, due to reasons not attributed to the purchaser, the purchaser
shall recover the amount of LD, in lieu of penalty, @ 0.5% of the total
contract price, paid or payable to the Firm per week of delay or part
thereof up to the maximum of 10% of the total contract price.
Further, these shall be governed by the provisions of clause No. 18 of the
General Condition of Contract.

3.0 SCOPE OF WORK :
The broad scope of work shall consist of following but not limited to:
(a) Development of Vendor Bill Tracking Portal

The broad scope of the work is as under:

i) The vendor shall enter the bill details and consignee will initiate,
review, comment, approve, reject etc. the billing process. Further,
the accounts department member shall be able to review,
comment, approve, reject etc. in SAP-ERP which will be reflected
on portal.

ii) For every bill details entered in portal should have the
corresponding SAP system generated PO number and option to
upload supporting documents, against that particular bill in the
portal.

iii) Portal will generate mail notification to the vendor of approval
/rejection as entered by consignee/accounts of RVPN. Portal will
also generate mail notification to approving officer in hierarchy.

iv) The vendor should be able to view/take print out of the
information like all PO/WO placed upon them, view pending
/passed invoices, balance hold, payment advice etc.

(b) Submission of online return of RVPN to GST portal ‘

The broad scope of the work is as under:

Data from SAP ERP system shall be fetched by System Integrator (SI)

and data will be transferred on Govt. portal with proper and secure

manner using credentials. Before, fetching the data from SAP ERP
system, various new database tables shall be created in SAP system
and in these newly created tables, data from various existing tables in

SAP system shall be populated on regular schedule. This process

needs customization as well as configuration part, further new reports

shall be required to be developed. Authorized users will be able

transfer the data from RVPN SAP system to matching format available

at RISL, Jaipur using a program / SAP connector. Required reports

. <ceuyae NO yere to be developed by the firm.

8.0 TERMS OF PAYMENTS
100% payment shall be made after completion of each task/milestone as mentioned below and upon verification from concerned module in-charge and
work-In-charge.
The firm's request for payment shall be made to the Purchaser in writing, accompanied by an invoice (in triplicate) with relevant supporting documents.
The payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of submission of an invoice/claim complete in all respects & supported by the requisite documents and
fulfilment of stipulated conditions & contractual formalities by the supplier after due verification by the Purchaser, who shall forward the same to the Sr.AO
(CPC), RVPN, Jaipur.
All payments shall be made to the Supplier by the Sr. AO (CPC), RVPN, Jaipur through RTGS/NEFT. The charges for transfer through RTGS/NEFT shall be on
the part of the bidder. No interest shall be paid on delayed payments.
The firm shall raise invoice on completion of milestones activities (as applicable), which are listed below:
S.No. Milestone/deliverables Paymentias per the Clause No.(1))
1 Completion of Design Phase (BBP) 30%
2. Go-live of PF Integration 30%
3 Go-live of STOMS integration 30%
4 Completion of Post Go-live support 10%
9.0 TRAINING
VPN requires end user training for technical officers/officials. The training shall be carried out at Jaipur Location. The appropriate duration may be
mutually decided by RVPN & firm. Training facilities such as hardware, software, furniture, refreshment etc. shall be provided by RVPN. Training period will
not be considered in completion period of work (clause No. 6. of this WO) and it may exceed the completion period. Necessary manual and procedure
documentation shall be provided by the firm.
10.0 TESTING
The firm should define the Integration test scripts. These test scripts will be used for testing, based on review and approval of RVPN. The firm should
populate sample master data in the system and configure the data integration. The firm shall undertake the testing of the data conversion and migration to
SAP system. The firm shall undertake the complete testing of the entire new system. The Integration testing will be done jointly by the firm's
implementation ‘gn and the RVPN core team.
17.0 USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING
The firm shall définé the User Acceptance Test (UAT) scripts. The Purchaser will approve the UAT test scripts. User Acceptance Testing (UAT) will be
] performed bathe PN, in accordance with approved UAT plan.

8.0 TERMS OF PAYMENTS
100% payment shall be made after completion of each man-month as per
scope of work as mentioned above and upon verification from concerned
module in-charge and work-In-charge.
The firm’s request for payment shall be made to the Purchaser in writing,
accompanied by an invoice (in triplicate) with relevant supporting
documents.
The payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of submission of an
invoice/claim complete in all respects & supported by the requisite
documents and fulfilment of stipulated conditions & contractual formalities
by the supplier after due verification by the Purchaser, who shall forward the
same to the Sr. AO (CPC).
All payments shall be made to the Supplier by the Sr. AO (CPC), RVPN,
Jaipur. Payment shall be made to the firm through RTGS/NEFT. The
charges for transfer through RTGS /NEFT shall be on the part of the bidder.
No interest shall be paid on delayed payments.
9.0 OTHER TERMS AND CONDITIONS
The other terms and condition for the above work shall remain same as
mentioned in the work order already placed on the firm vide this office No.
817 dated 22.12.2014

the other terms and conditions agreed to.

/ may be initially valid for a period of 3(three) years from the date of completion of
' stabilization period which is required to be extended by the Contractor one month prior

; for the Bank Guarantee for Contract performance is enclosed at Section -VIII of this bid

Professional Manpower service in SAP

SPECIAL CONDITIONS OF CONTRACT
DESCRIPTION OF WORK/ SERVICE: SAP Technical Support in ABAP
Programming and Functional Support in SAP modules (MM/FICO/PS/PM/SD)
Numaligarh Refinery Limited (NRL) has implemented ERP - SAP ECC 6.0 for the
business processes in the following modules: FICO, MM, PS, PM, SD, HR including
ESS/MSS Portal.
OBJECTIVE:
NRL intends to outsource SAP Technical support in ABAP programming and
Functional support in SAP modules per “man-day" basis for a period of one year.
The deployed resources must have adequate functional knowledge/experience in SAP
modules i.e, MM, PS, SD, FICO, PM and HR. The Job will be onsite/offsite based on
nature /requirement of the job by mutually agreed by NRL and the Bidder.
The scope of work shall, inter-alia, include but not be limited to the following:
1. ABAP Programming:
In MM, PS, SD, FICO, PM, HR modules
# Enhancement framework, user exits, BAD! and dialog programming/module pool,
web-dynpro in ABAP
# Conversions using BAPI, BDC
# Reporting in ALV, Interactive and list.
# Analyzing Standard Transactions with Debugging
# Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) and Application Link Enabling (ALE)
Communication with Legacy Systems.
# Any development in ABAP related to MM, PS, SD, FICO, PM, HR modules
ELIGIBILITY OF ABAP PROGRAMMER
The ABAP Programmer should be a BE/ MCA/ B.Tech. and SAP Certified ABAP
Consultant. He/she should have 3 years post certification ABAP programming
experience.
The ABAP Programmer should have expertise in:
aka -y #% All the RICEFW (Reports, Interfaces, Conversions, Enhancements, Forms,
i ~” Workflows) components.
-G VF OW # ABAP Tools: SAP Query, Report Writer, Report Painter, Workflow, Menu Painter,
Ya. UY Yl Screen Painter and Function Modules.
——<- # ABAP Data Dictionary: Tables, Include Structures, Domain, Data Elements,
Structure, Views, Index, Search Help and Lock Objects.
# Reporting: Classical/Interactive Reports, ALV Reports.
# Interfaces: Business Application Programming Interface (BAPI) using Business
Object Repository (BOR), EDI, ALE/IDoc, RFC Function Modules.
# Conversions: BDC, LSMW
# Enhancements: User-Exits, CMOD/SMOD Function-Exits, BADI, BTE , BDT
—_— # Forms: SAP Script, Smart forms, Adobe Forms.
DATA? # Domain knowledge in FICO, MM, PM, PS.
, ~ TA "> # Proficient in Dialog Program (Module Pool-Dialog Programming) using screen

SCOPE OF WORK:
The ABAP Programmers have to work on all the ABAP jobs assigned to them during
the contract period.
They have to work as follows:
i) Functional Specification (FS) of all the Development / Modification jobs will be
prepared by respective functional team and forwarded to ABAP team lead (NRL) in the
format of SAP Request Form.
ii) ABAP team lead will assign jobs to the ABAP Programmer (Vendor / Consultant).
iil) The ABAP Programmer will do development as per the FS and should complete the
same within mutually agreed time between NRL and the vendor. He/she may consult
with the respective functional team member for any clarification regarding the business
process.
iv) On completion of development/modification, same will be given for unit testing.
v) On successful completion of unit testing the ABAP Programmer will release the
development for transporting to production. Otherwise, he/she will restart the process
from step No iii) above.
vi) ABAP programmers help may be needed during testing.
vii) After successful release of the job, the ABAP Programmers have to prepare the
Technical Specification (TS) in the same SAP Request Form and hand it over to ABAP
team lead.
viii) The job assigned to the programmer will be considered as closed if the same is
successfully transported to PRD, it meets the requirements as described in the FS
document and the completed TS in the same SAP Request Form is signed off by
ABAP Team Lead of NRL.
2. Functional Support (in MM,PS,SD,FICO,PM)
# Customization, report configuration, new functionalities, new processes etc.
4 j # SAP Note , Patch upload

Overview of Bill Tracking Web Application:
NRL has introduced an automated Bill Receipt & Tracking System -
TRackOne in website www.nrl.co.in facilitating our vendors to access
information relating to their transactions with NRL.
TRackOne application allows vendors to check the status of invoices and
track the status of payments.
Salient details of the system are as under:
1) All POs generated henceforth will bear the list of documents
required to be submitted by the vendor along with the Bill.
You are required to submit the following details along with Bill
submission
1 . CONTRACT AGREEMENT
2 . SERVICE TAX INVOICE-FORM 4(A)/ORIGINAL INVOICE
2) All suppliers, vendors and contractors are enabled through
www.nrl.co.in to track the status of their bills. All they have to do
is to etenter the Tracking No received via sms/email and the system
will inform about details status of their bills.
3) You are required to send your bills in a closed envelop invariably
mentioning the following information on the face of it.
INVOICE NO. & DATE: eater

accepted for payment at site.

execution of the work including submission of bills. Period of this contract is noted in subsequent sheets forming
part of this contract document.

non-judicial Stamp paper of appropriate value. The contract shall be signed by you/your authorised

receipt of this order.

so as to execute the job as recommended by the EIC in all respects without any additional cost to the owner.

The contractor will not engage minor labour below 18 years of age under any circumstances and will fulfill and
comply all the provisions of applicable act and indemnify the company against all claims, which may arise out of
such Acts & Rules framed there under, The Acts and Rules to be abided by are ‘The Contract Labour (Regulation
and Abolition) Act’, ‘The minimum wages Act’, ‘The Workman's Compensation Act’, ‘The Payment of Wages Act’,
‘The Payment of Bonus Act’, 'The Employees Provident Fund & Misc. Provisions Act’, 'Inter State Migrant
Workmen (Regulation of Employment & Condition of Service) Act' or any other Acts or Statute not here in above
specifically mentioned having bearing over engagement of workers directly or indirectly for execution of work and
submit copies of the same wherever necessary to the Engineer-in-charge before start of the work.
7. SAFETY, SECURITY & ENVIRONMENT:
All statutory & safety/security norms and regulations followed by NRL will be fully complied too. Any work in the
Refinery shall be covered through work / safety permits. It will be contractors’ responsibility to provide all safety
gadgets specified for the work.

the date of receipt. Please note that your work will start only after furnishing all the documents as sought and

TO WHOMSOEVER IT MAY CONCERN

This is to certify that since 04.05.2015 M/s. Yash Technologies Private Limited, Indore has been

providing functional & technical support/enhancements/developments in ABAP programming (ABAP,

ABAP-HR, WebDynpro, Workflow and BASIS) in the mentioned modules of SAP

(MM/FICO/PS/PM/SD/HR-Payroll/ESS&MSS_IS-Oil/ TRM/ BPC/BIBO) and interfacing with other

non-SAP systems/software as per following work orders:

1. WO - 4300039950 Dated:: 04.05.2015
2. WO-4300056447 Dated::11.09.2018

The total SAP user base of in NRL is 970 (including ECC, BPC & BO).

We also hereby confirm with reference to work order no 4300039950 M/s. Yash Technologies has

implemented and supported SAP TRM(Treasury Risk Management) starting August 2016 & Go-live on

February 2017.

SAP® PartnerEdge™ Channel Agreement VAR
Discount Letter
{not applicable for SAP Business One and SAP BusinessObjects Portfolio)

This Discount Letter (“Discount Letter’) represenis an amendment to the SAP®
PartnerEdge ™Channel Agreement VAR (“Agreement’), entered by and between SAP India
Private Limited, with principal offices at Wing A. 2"? Floor, Tower B, Salarpuria Softzone,
Sarjapur Outer Ring Road, Sellandur Post. Bangalore 560 103, Kamataka, India, (“SAP”) and
YASH Technologies Pvt Ltd, wilh principal offices at 201-205, Bansi Trade Center.
581/5, M G Road, Indore - MP 452001, India (‘Partner’)
This Discount Letter becomes effective on 15th August 2011 and is valid until the expiry or
terrnination of the PartnerEdge Channel Agreement VAR ( whichever is earlier), unless SAP.
gives notice to the Partner of its desire to terrninate this discount letter no later than 30 days
prior to any scheduled expiration date.
This Discount Letter is part of the SAP® PartnerEdge"™ Channel Agreement VAR for SAP
Business All-in-One, and does not apply to any other partner program
No maintenance discounts are granted under this Discount Letter
A. Definitions
4. PartnerEdge classification ‘

SAP classifies pariner as a Gold. Silver or Bronze Partner Partner ‘evel

classifications and the criteria for attaining them are specified in the PartnerEdge

Program Guide.

In the event the Partner level changes during the term of this letter, the relevant

provisions of the PartnerEdge program apply.

Changes to Partner level take effect according to rules specified in the PartnerEdge

Program Guide
B. Discounts
With exception of the Volume discount, the discounts described in this Discount Letter shall
be calculated based on the manufacturer's suggested retail price (‘MSRP’). The MSRP
equals the SAP Price List minus any applicable volume discount
1. Volume Discount

« SAP grants to Partner a Volume discounl expressed as a percentage off the

Price List, based on the revenue generated by sale of SAP Software products.
e« Volume discounts are determined according to the total list price license fee in a
given deal.

2. Standard PartnerEdge VAR Discount

« SAP grants to Partner a discount based on Partner's then current program level

(Gold, Silver, Bronze) as set out below

4. Maintenance Resale Discount (applicable for SAP Business All-in-One solutions

discount
SAP Standard Support 1%

« “Qualified Assistance” shall comprise of the following activities and/or any other
activity as defined by SAP from time-to-time
© Partner advises End User on possible maintenance offerings of SAP
o Partner provides End User with the current and applicable SAP
maintenance agreement template
o Partner informs End User of applicable pricing for maintenance in
accordance with information and/or tools provided by SAP
© Partner provides End User data in electronic or written forin as requested
by SAP to SAP
o Partner handles End User signed agreements and forwards them to SAP.

e« Based on the accurate filled SAP maintenance agreement template, SAP will
decide whether to accept or refuse the offer by End User: in the first case by
signing and returning the signed maintenance agreement for SAP Delivered
Support to End User. It shall be in SAP's sole discretion to enter into a

*« The Maintenance Resale Discount shall be applied only if Partner follows all
procedures identified by SAP to Partner on how to provide Qualified Assistance,
which includes, e.g, that any maintenance order for SAP Delivered Support is
provided simultaneously with Partner's respective Software license order. SAP
may decide that when Partner provides a templale agreement validly executed by
End User, this shall be corisidered sufficient proof that Partner provided Qualified
Assistance.
« The Maintenance Resale Discount shall become applicable as soon as SAP
enters into the maintenance agreement with End User due to Partner providing
Qualified Assistance SAP shall not have any claims for additional payment (i.e
the Maintenance Resale Discount will not be revoked retroactively) if End User
does not fulfill End User's obligations under the relevant maintenance agreement
between SAP and End User.
The one time Maintenance Resale Discount shall be Partner's exclusive compensation for
handling of or facilitating condusion of the maintenance agreement between SAP and End
User, so it is a one-time discount and not applicable on, e.g., renewal of SAP delivered
Support after the initial term. Partner shall not have any claims for further compensation if
SAP or any third party sells additional Software, Services or Maintenance Services to the End
User.
C. General
1. SAP grants to Partner discounts for the Software products listed in the applicable Price
List As sct out above. not all discounts may be available for all Software products and
may vary by product The discounts may apply curnulatively unless stated otherwise
above
2. SAP grants to Partner a discount on third party products included in the applicable and
then current SAP price list provided that such third party products are marked as eligible
in the SAP price list. The applicable discounts are sct out on such price list.
3. Certain discounts for SAP software products granted by SAP depend on the program
level of Partner in the PartnerEdge Program (Gold, Silver and Bronze). If the program
level changes during the validity period of this Discount Letter in accordance with the
tules described in the Program Guide, the discount granted to Partner will be adjusted
accordingly.
4. for any checks under this Discount Letter, SAP will determine annual turnovers and/or
number of employees of a customer based on commercial information provided by Dun &
Bradstreet or any similarly reliable commercial information service in SAP sole discretion,
including in particular written evidence to be provided by Partner.

automated quotation tools shall be subject to SAP’s check of Partner's eligibility to such

partner, which SAP may change from time to time.

SAP® PartnerEdge™ Channel Agreement VAR
Discount Letter
(not applicable for SAP Business All-in-One and SAP Business One Portfolio)
This Discount Letter (‘Discount Letter’) represents an amendment to the SAP® PartnerEdge™
For Channel Partners for the SAP® BusinessObjects™ Portfolio - EXHIBIT A TO SOLUTION
PROVIDER AGREEMENT (“Agreement’}, entered by and between SAP India Private Limited,
with principal offices at Wing A, 2° Floor, Tower B, Salarpuria Softzone, Sarjapur Outer Ring
Road, Beliandur Post, Bangalore 560 103, Kamataka, India, (“SAP”) and YASH
Technologies Pvt Ltd, with principal offices at 201-205, Bans! Trade Center,
581/5, M. G. Road, Indore - MP 452001, India (‘Partner’)
This Discount Letter becomes effective on 15” August 2011 and is valid until the expiry or
termination of the PartnerEdge Channel Agreement VAR ( whichever is earlier), unless SAP
gives notice to the Partner of its desire to terminate this discount letter no later than 30 days
prior to any scheduled expiration date.
This Discount Letter is part of the SAP® PartnerEdge™ Channel Agreement VAR for SAP
BusinessObjects, and does not apply to any other partner program
No maintenance discounts are granted under this Discount Letter
A. Definitions
4. PartnerEdge classification
SAP classifies partner as a Gold, Silver or Bronze Partner. Partner level
classifications and the criteria for attaining them are specified in the PartnerEdge
Program Guide.
In the event the Partner level changes during the termn of this letter, the relevant
provisions of the PartnerEdge program apply.
Changes to Partner level iake effect according to rules specified in the PartnerEdge
Program Guide
B. Discounts
The discounts described in this Discount Letter shall be caiculated based on the
manufacturer’s suggested retail pce (‘MSRP’).
2. Standard PartnerEdge VAR Discount
e SAP grants to Partner a discount based on Pariner’s then current program level
(Gold, Silver, Bronze) as set out below

discounts apply:

3. Maintenance Resale Discount (applicable for SAP BusinessObjects solutions only}
« SAP grants an additional Maintenance Resale Discount for all sales of SAP SAP
BusinessObjects Software, if Partner provides Qualified Assistance (as defined
below) to SAP by establishing a direct agreement between SAP and End User for
maintenance for the SAP Software sold by Partner (so called "SAP Delivered
Support’).
Note for clarification. The Maintenance Resale Discount aiso applies on the MSRP and is
therefore a discount granted on the software product
2 The amount of additional percentage points based on the MSRP varies by the
support offering that SAP contracts with the respective End User
SAP Support Offering Additional percentage points of
discount
SAP Standard Support 1%
SAP Enterprise Support 2%
e “Qualified Assistance” shall comprise of the following activities and/or any other
activity as defined by SAP from time-to-time:
© Partner advises End User on possible maintenance offerings of SAP
o Partner provides End User with the current and applicable SAP
maintenance agreement template
o Partner informs End User of applicable pricing for maintenance in
accordance with information and/or tools provided by SAP
o Partner provides End User data in electronic or written form as requested
by SAP to SAP
o Partner handles End User signed agreements and forwards them to SAP.
« Based on the accurate filled SAP maintenance agreement template, SAP will
decide whether to accept or refuse the offer by End User, in the first case by
signing and returming the signed maintenance agreement for SAP Delivered
Support to End User It shali be in SAP’s sole discretion to enter into a
maintenance agreement with End User(s) Partner shall not have any claims if
SAP decides not to enter into a maintenance agreement for a specific End User,
regardless of any effort incurred by Partner in this regard.
» The Maintenance Resale Discount shali be applied only if Partner follows ali
procedures identified by SAP to Pariner on how to provide Qualified Assistance,
which includes, e.g., that any maintenance order for SAP Delivered Support is
provided simultaneously with Partner's respective Software license order. SAP
may decide that when Partner provides a template agreement validly executed by
End User, this shal! be considered sufficient proof that Partner provided Qualified
Assistance.
